title: How do endurance runners actually train? Relationship with competition performance
claim_impact: Supports descriptive links between training organization and performance only; no nonweekly or 9.5-day comparison

title: Training Session Models in Endurance Sports: A Norwegian Perspective on Best Practice Recommendations
claim_impact: Adds component and session-model vocabulary but cannot validate a 9.5-day frame or youth prescription

title: Training variability and threshold density: a conceptual comparison of East African and Norwegian endurance training systems
claim_impact: Reinforces that successful systems vary in session density; no exact cycle-length trial and no youth safety authority

title: Fatigue and Recovery Time Course After Female Soccer Matches: A Systematic Review And Meta-analysis
claim_impact: Separates performance perceptual and biochemical recovery timelines; contradicts one recovery-complete timestamp

title: The Effect of Exercise-Induced Muscle Damage on Lower Limb Side Cut Biomechanics and Task Achievement in Male and Female Team Sport Athletes
claim_impact: Adds sex-inclusive 48-hour biomechanics evidence but does not transfer to adolescent middle-distance MAIN clearance

title: The Effects of Match-Play on Markers of Recovery in Professional Female and Male Soccer Players
claim_impact: Shows marker- and sex-specific residual effects at day three; strengthens prohibition on treating 72 hours as universal clearance

title: Physical and Mental Recovery for Female Footballers: Considerations and Approaches for Better Practice
claim_impact: Adds female-specific recovery context while explicitly retaining individual variability and incomplete evidence

title: Effect of Sequencing Strength and Endurance Training in Young Male Soccer Players
claim_impact: Suggests limited sequence differences in one youth soccer protocol; cannot become a universal Formation order rule

title: The effects of concurrent training on physical fitness in children and adolescents: a systematic review and meta-analysis
claim_impact: Updates youth concurrent-training evidence but high heterogeneity and an age range extending to 24 block exact sequence and spacing rules

title: Perspectives from research and practice: A survey on external load monitoring and bone in sport
claim_impact: Supports provenance and construct separation; survey perceptions cannot establish bone-stress risk thresholds

title: Monitoring Training Effects in Athletes: A Multidimensional Framework for Decision-Making
claim_impact: Supports separating load state and response but its pragmatic two-session example must not become a universal baseline minimum

title: Statistical Primer for Athletic Trainers: The Essentials of Understanding Measures of Reliability and Minimal Important Change
claim_impact: Supports metric-specific measurement error and minimal detectable change; does not validate a readiness threshold

title: What Works Clearinghouse Procedures and Standards Handbook Version 5.0
claim_impact: Updates official single-case design standards; no universal observation count for uncontrolled athlete diaries

title: Examining and Enhancing the Methodological Quality of Nonconcurrent Multiple-Baseline Designs
claim_impact: Requires replication and randomization upgrades; weakens causal claims from simple before-after athlete logs

title: Single-case experimental designs to assess intervention effectiveness in rehabilitation: A practical guide
claim_impact: Supports repeated measurement sequential introduction fidelity and design-specific analysis; no sport-specific minimum n

title: Combining nonoverlap and trend for single-case research: Tau-U
claim_impact: Offers one trend-aware effect-size method but does not solve carryover confounding or design weakness

title: A new framework for developing and evaluating complex interventions: update of Medical Research Council guidance
claim_impact: Supports iterative feasibility context and stakeholder evaluation before effectiveness claims

title: Applying Human Factors and Usability Engineering to Medical Devices
claim_impact: Supports use-error analysis and representative-user testing; TRAINORACLE is not thereby classified as a medical device

title: Reporting guidelines for clinical trial reports for interventions involving artificial intelligence: the CONSORT-AI extension
claim_impact: Adds algorithm version human interaction and error reporting for later comparative trials; not a pilot conduct standard

title: FUTURE-AI: international consensus guideline for trustworthy and deployable artificial intelligence in healthcare
claim_impact: Reinforces end-user involvement traceability usability oversight and lifecycle monitoring; no sport prescription efficacy

title: Week-to-week changes in training were not prospectively associated with injuries among Wisconsin high school cross-country runners
claim_impact: Directly contradicts automatic injury-risk rules based only on weekly volume duration intensity or training-stress change

title: Changes in Sleep Stress and Fatigue Were Not Prospectively Associated With Running-Related Injuries Among High School Cross Country Runners
claim_impact: Directly blocks converting short-term self-report changes into an injury prediction or clearance score

title: Prevalence and burden of health problems in competitive adolescent distance runners: A 6-month prospective cohort study
claim_impact: Supports participant-visible concern capture and handoff because athletes may train while health problems are present; no causal threshold

title: Co-creation of injury prevention measures for competitive adolescent distance runners: knowledge behavior and needs of athletes and coaches enrolled on England Athletics' Youth Talent Programme
claim_impact: Supports athlete-coach co-design and context-specific delivery; does not validate an automated safety rule
